entries.title: Camp Attack Leaves Students Injured And Restricted

context.label: Training camp aftermath creates institutional crisis

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After the camp attack, students are injured, monitored, and restricted while adults handle the kidnapping crisis.
content.injection: In post-camp scenes, keep students shaken and constrained by adults; they are not officially authorized to mount a rescue.
constraints: Hospital recovery and adult restrictions should shape pacing.
antiLore: Do not have U.A. casually send students after Bakugo.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Students Feel Guilt But Lack Authority

context.label: Students recover under guilt and restriction

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.

content.fact: Several students feel responsible for Bakugo’s capture, but they have no legal authority to perform a rescue operation.
content.injection: When students discuss saving Bakugo, frame the impulse as emotionally understandable but legally and professionally dangerous.
constraints: Iida should strongly understand the danger after Hosu.
antiLore: Do not portray the student rescue plan as fully sanctioned by U.A.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Kirishima Has The Strongest Personal Pull To Reach Bakugo

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kirishima’s relationship with Bakugo gives him a uniquely credible chance to reach Bakugo during the rescue extraction.
content.injection: In rescue-planning scenes, let Kirishima’s bond with Bakugo carry practical weight; he is not just another classmate in the extraction plan.
constraints: Bakugo is more likely to accept Kirishima’s hand than most classmates.
antiLore: Do not make Izuku the only emotionally relevant rescuer for Bakugo.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Iida Opposes Recklessness Because Hosu Changed Him

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Iida’s opposition to the rescue plan is shaped by his own near-fatal illegal revenge attempt in Hosu.
content.injection: When Iida challenges the rescue group, write him as someone who learned the cost of acting outside hero authority, not as a coward or obstruction.
constraints: He may still join to monitor and prevent worse recklessness.
antiLore: Do not ignore Iida’s Stain arc growth during the rescue debate.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Yaoyorozu Tracker Makes Rescue Possible But Ethically Fraught

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Yaoyorozu’s tracker provides the students with a way to locate the villains, but using it outside adult authority is ethically and professionally fraught.
content.injection: Use the tracker as a serious decision point: it enables action, but it also places students in conflict with lawful hero procedure.
constraints: Momo must not treat the choice lightly.
antiLore: Do not make the tracker a consequence-free adventure hook.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Todoroki Joins Rescue From Shared Failure And Practical Resolve

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Todoroki joins the rescue effort from guilt, friendship, and practical resolve after failing to prevent Bakugo’s capture.
content.injection: In the rescue group, portray Todoroki as controlled and serious, not impulsively heroic for spectacle.
constraints: He understands danger but still chooses to go.
antiLore: Do not make Todoroki indifferent to Bakugo’s capture.

entries.title: U.A. Faces Public Blame For Endangering Students

context.label: Training camp aftermath creates institutional crisis

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo’s kidnapping from a U.A. training camp creates a public trust crisis for the school and hero society.
content.injection: After the camp attack, include public and parental pressure on U.A.; the school must answer for student safety, not just prepare the next battle.
constraints: Officials should care about optics and safety policy.
antiLore: Do not treat U.A.’s reputation as unchanged after the kidnapping.

entries.title: Bakugo Rescue Plan Must Avoid Direct Combat

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The students’ plan is to locate and extract Bakugo without directly fighting villains, because combat would repeat the illegal mistake of Hosu.
content.injection: For the student rescue group, keep the plan explicitly noncombat: observe, reach Bakugo if possible, and avoid engaging villains.
constraints: If the students fight, it should be framed as a serious breach, not normal hero work.
antiLore: Do not write the rescue squad as officially deputized combatants.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

content.fact: U.A. must answer publicly for the camp attack and kidnapping, turning hero education into a media accountability crisis.
content.injection: After the camp attack, include press and public accountability pressure on U.A.; the institution is under scrutiny.
constraints: School authority figures should respond formally.
antiLore: Do not keep U.A. public reputation untouched.

entries.title: Bakugo Rescue Squad Is Small Because Secrecy Matters

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The student rescue group stays small because broader involvement would increase risk, conflict, and exposure.
content.injection: Keep the unauthorized rescue group limited and tense; not all classmates should agree or be included.
constraints: The group’s secrecy creates interpersonal strain.
antiLore: Do not have all of Class 1-A march to Kamino.

entries.title: Momo Is Torn Between Rule And Rescue

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis

content.fact: Momo’s tracker puts her between trust in adult procedure and classmates’ need to act.
content.injection: When Momo discusses the tracker, give her hesitation and responsibility; she must not be reckless or indifferent.

content.fact: Class 1-A is not unified around the unauthorized rescue plan; disagreement over ethics and risk matters.
content.injection: In rescue-plan scenes, allow disagreement and tension inside Class 1-A instead of unanimous adventure-party consent.
